sentences.zh: 臣誠恐身塗野草，尸并卒伍，故數上書求見，輒報罷。

translations.literal: Your servant truly fears his body smeared on the wild grass, his corpse ranked among the ranks—therefore he has repeatedly memorialized asking audience, and each time is answered with dismissal.
translations.idiomatic: I risk the execution ground yet keep petitioning—and each time you brush me off.

sentences.zh: 臣聞齊桓之時有以九九見者，桓公不逆，欲以致大也。

translations.literal: Your servant has heard that in Duke Huan of Qi's time someone came with the multiplication table; Huan did not reject him, wishing thereby to draw in the great.
translations.idiomatic: Duke Huan once heard a man teach the times table and still received him—hunger for greatness.

sentences.zh: 〈師古曰：「九九，筭術，若今九章、五曹之輩。」

translations.literal: 〈Yan's note: "Jiu jiu is reckoning, like today's Nine Chapters or Five Offices manuals."〉
translations.idiomatic: Yan Shigu: jiu jiu is arithmetic, like later mathematical primers.

sentences.zh: 〉今臣所言非特九九也，陛下距臣者三矣，此天下士所以不至也。

translations.literal: 〉What your servant speaks is more than the multiplication table—yet thrice Your Majesty has shut me out; that is why knights of the realm do not come.
translations.idiomatic: My words outweigh a times table—three rebuffs prove why talent stays home.

sentences.zh: 昔秦武王好力，任鄙叩關自鬻；

translations.literal: Formerly King Wu of Qin loved strength; Ren Bi knocked at the gate to sell himself;
translations.idiomatic: King Wu of Qin loved brawn; Ren Bi pounded the gate to offer his back;

sentences.zh: 〈師古曰：「秦武王即孝公之孫，惠文王之子也。

translations.literal: 〈Yan's note: "King Wu of Qin is the grandson of Duke Xiao, son of King Huiwen."〉
translations.idiomatic: Yan Shigu identifies the Qin king.

sentences.zh: 任鄙，力士也。」

translations.literal: Ren Bi was a strongman."〉
translations.idiomatic: Ren Bi was a noted strongman."

sentences.zh: 〉繆公行伯，繇余歸德。

translations.literal: 〉Duke Mu acted as hegemon; You Yu came over out of virtue.
translations.idiomatic: Duke Mu sought hegemony; You Yu defected out of respect.

sentences.zh: 〈師古曰：「即秦穆公。

translations.literal: 〈Yan's note: "That is Duke Mu of Qin."
translations.idiomatic: Yan Shigu: Qin Mugong.

sentences.zh: 伯讀曰霸。

translations.literal: Bo is read ba.
translations.idiomatic: Bo here reads ba, hegemon.

sentences.zh: 繇讀曰由。」

translations.literal: You is read you."〉
translations.idiomatic: You is read you."

sentences.zh: 〉今欲致天下之士，民有上書求見者，輒使詣尚書問其所言，言可采取者，秩以升斗之祿，賜以一束之帛。

translations.literal: 〉Now if you wish to draw knights of the realm, let anyone who submits a memorial asking audience be sent to the Imperial Secretary to be asked what he says; if the words may be adopted, give him rank with a few piculs of salary and grant a bundle of silk.
translations.idiomatic: To summon talent, route every memorial to the Imperial Secretary; pay useful words with a token stipend and a bolt of silk.

sentences.zh: 若此，則天下之士發憤懣，〈師古曰：「懣音滿。」

translations.literal: If so, then knights of the realm will vent loyal resentment—〈Yan's note: "Men is read man."〉
translations.idiomatic: Then honest rage will pour forth—Yan Shigu gives fanqie for men.

sentences.zh: 〉吐忠言，嘉謀日聞於上，天下條貫，國家表裏，爛然可睹矣。

translations.literal: 〉pour out loyal words, and good plans daily reach the ruler; the realm's threads, the state's inner and outer, will blaze clear to sight.
translations.idiomatic: Good counsel will flow upward until every fiber of the realm lies plain.

sentences.zh: 〈師古曰：「爛然，分明之貌也。」

translations.literal: 〈Yan's note: "Ran ran means the appearance of clarity."〉
translations.idiomatic: Yan Shigu: ran ran means blazingly clear.

sentences.zh: 〉夫以四海之廣，士民之數，能言之類至衆多也。

translations.literal: 〉Within the four seas' breadth and the people's numbers, the articulate sort are exceedingly many.
translations.idiomatic: Eloquent men swarm the four seas.

sentences.zh: 然其儁桀指世陳政，言成文章，質之先聖而不繆，〈師古曰：「質，正也。」

translations.literal: Yet those who point at the age and lay out policy, whose words form patterned essays, checked against the former sages without error—〈Yan's note: "Zhi means to correct."〉
translations.idiomatic: Yet few who write like classics and pass the sages' test—Yan Shigu: zhi is "test against."

sentences.zh: 〉施之當世合時務，若此者，亦亡幾人。

translations.literal: 〉applied to the present fit the times—such men are also not many.
translations.idiomatic: Fewer still fit the present moment.

sentences.zh: 〈師古曰：「無幾，言不多也。

translations.literal: 〈Yan's note: "Wu ji means not many.
translations.idiomatic: Yan Shigu: wu ji means scarce.

sentences.zh: 幾音居豈反。」

translations.literal: Ji is read ju-qi fan."〉
translations.idiomatic: Fanqie for ji."

sentences.zh: 〉故爵祿束帛者，天下之厎石，〈師古曰：「厎，細石也，音之履反，又音秖。」

translations.literal: 〉Therefore rank, stipend, and bundles of silk are the realm's whetstone—〈Yan's note: "Di is fine stone; read zhi-li fan, also read zhi."〉
translations.idiomatic: So stipend and silk are the empire's grindstone—Yan Shigu glosses di as fine whetstone.

sentences.zh: 〉高祖所以厲世摩鈍也。

translations.literal: 〉this is how Gaozu sharpened the age and honed the blunt.
translations.idiomatic: That is how Gaozu whetted the world's edge.

sentences.zh: 孔子曰：「工欲善其事，必先利其器。」

translations.literal: Confucius said, "The craftsman who wishes to do his work well must first sharpen his tools."
translations.idiomatic: Confucius said sharpen your tools before the job.

sentences.zh: 〈師古曰：「論語載孔子之言也。

translations.literal: 〈Yan's note: "The Analects records Confucius's words.
translations.idiomatic: Yan Shigu cites the Analects.

sentences.zh: 工以喻國政，利器喻賢材。」

translations.literal: The craftsman stands for state affairs; sharp tools stand for worthy talent."〉
translations.idiomatic: The craftsman is government; the tools are ministers."

sentences.zh: 〉至秦則不然，張誹謗之罔，以爲漢𢿛除，倒持泰阿，授楚其柄。

translations.literal: 〉Qin did otherwise: it spread a net of slander laws, thinking to clear the way for Han, yet held the Tai'e sword by the blade and handed Chu the hilt.
translations.idiomatic: Qin stretched slander statutes to pave Han's rise—then gripped the Tai'e blade by the edge and gave the hilt to rebels.

sentences.zh: 〈師古曰：「泰阿，劔名，歐冶所鑄也。

translations.literal: 〈Yan's note: "Tai'e is a sword name, forged by Ou Yezi."〉
translations.idiomatic: Yan Shigu: Tai'e was Ou Yezi's master blade.

sentences.zh: 言秦無道，令陳涉、項羽乘間而發，譬倒持劔而以把授與人也。」

translations.literal: It means Qin was lawless, letting Chen She and Xiang Yu seize the moment—like holding a sword upside down and offering the grip to another."〉
translations.idiomatic: Lawless Qin let Chen She and Xiang Yu strike—an empire handing enemies the sword grip.

sentences.zh: 〉故誠能勿失其柄，天下雖有不順，莫敢觸其鋒，此孝武皇帝所以辟地建功爲漢世宗也。

translations.literal: 〉Therefore if you truly do not lose the hilt, though there be unruly men under Heaven none dare touch the edge—this is how Emperor Wu opened lands and built merit to become Han's Exalted Ancestor.
translations.idiomatic: Keep the hilt and no rebel tests the edge—that is how Wu became the dynasty's martial ancestor.

sentences.zh: 〈師古曰：「辟讀曰闢。」

translations.literal: 〈Yan's note: "Pi is read pi 'to open.'"〉
translations.idiomatic: Yan Shigu reads pi as open or expand.

sentences.zh: 〉今不循伯者之道，〈師古曰：「伯讀曰霸。

translations.literal: 〉Now, not following the hegemon's path—〈Yan's note: "Bo is read ba.
translations.idiomatic: Today, ignoring the hegemon's way—Yan Shigu: bo reads ba.

sentences.zh: 次下亦同。」

translations.literal: What follows below is the same."〉
translations.idiomatic: The same reading recurs below."

sentences.zh: 〉欲以三代選舉之法取當時之士，猶察伯樂之圖，求騏驥於市，而不可得，亦已明矣。

translations.literal: 〉yet wishing to take the Three Dynasties' election methods to recruit men of the present is like studying Bo Le's horse chart to seek a swift steed in the market—unobtainable; it is already clear.
translations.idiomatic: Using ancient selection rites to hire modern men is like finding a thoroughbred from a picture—hopeless and obvious.

sentences.zh: 故高祖棄陳平之過而獲其謀，〈師古曰：「盜嫂受金之事。」

translations.literal: Therefore Gaozu overlooked Chen Ping's faults and gained his stratagems—〈Yan's note: "The affair of stealing his sister-in-law and accepting gold."〉
translations.idiomatic: Gaozu pardoned Chen Ping's scandals and took his plots—Yan Shigu cites the old gossip.

sentences.zh: 〉晉文召天王，齊桓用其讎，〈師古曰：「召天王，謂狩于河陽也。

translations.literal: 〉King Wen of Jin summoned the Son of Heaven; Duke Huan of Qi employed his enemy—〈Yan's note: "Summoning the Son of Heaven means the hunt at Heyang."
translations.idiomatic: Jin Wen met the king at hunt; Huan used a foe as minister—Yan Shigu: the Heyang assembly.

sentences.zh: 用其讎，謂以管仲爲相。

translations.literal: Using his enemy means making Guan Zhong chancellor.
translations.idiomatic: The enemy was Guan Zhong, made chief minister.

sentences.zh: 並解於上。」

translations.literal: Both glossed above."〉
translations.idiomatic: Both stories glossed earlier."

sentences.zh: 〉亡益於時，不顧逆順，此所謂伯道者也。

translations.literal: 〉It brings no benefit to the times, ignores what is contrary or compliant—this is what is called the hegemon's way.
translations.idiomatic: It ignores expedience yet works—that is hegemon politics.

sentences.zh: 一色成體謂之醇，白黑雜合謂之駮。

translations.literal: One color forming a whole is called chun; white and black mixed is called bo.
translations.idiomatic: Pure hue is chun; mottled white and black is bo.

sentences.zh: 欲以承平之法治暴秦之緒，〈師古曰：「緒謂餘業也。」

translations.literal: To wish to govern the violent leavings of tyrant Qin with the statutes of full peace—〈Yan's note: "Xu means the remaining work."〉
translations.idiomatic: Applying Wen-era statutes to Qin's wreckage—Yan Shigu: xu is leftover evil.

sentences.zh: 〉猶以鄉飲酒之禮理軍市也。

translations.literal: 〉is like ordering a village drinking rite to settle an army market.
translations.idiomatic: It is like running a village banquet in a war camp.

sentences.zh: 今陛下旣不納天下之言，又加戮焉。

translations.literal: Now Your Majesty neither accepts the words of the realm nor adds execution on top.
translations.idiomatic: You reject counsel and pile executions on top.

sentences.zh: 夫䳒鵲遭害，則仁鳥增逝；

translations.literal: When owls and kites suffer harm, benevolent birds fly farther;
translations.idiomatic: When hawks and owls are harmed, the benevolent birds fly farther away;

sentences.zh: 〈師古曰：「䳒，鴟也。

translations.literal: 〈Yan's note: "You is the owl.
translations.idiomatic: Yan Shigu: you names the owl.

sentences.zh: 仁鳥，鸞鳳也。

translations.literal: Benevolent birds are luan and feng.
translations.idiomatic: Benevolent birds are luan and phoenix.

sentences.zh: 䳒音緣。」

translations.literal: You is read yuan."〉
translations.idiomatic: You is read yuan."

sentences.zh: 〉愚者蒙戮，則知士深退。

translations.literal: 〉when fools are struck down, wise men withdraw deep.
translations.idiomatic: Kill fools and the wise dig in.

sentences.zh: 〈師古曰：「蒙，被也。」

translations.literal: 〈Yan's note: "Meng means to suffer."〉
translations.idiomatic: Yan Shigu: meng means suffer.

sentences.zh: 〉間者愚民上疏，多觸不急之法，或下廷尉，而死者衆。

translations.literal: 〉Recently commoners have submitted memorials that often brush the "not urgent" statutes; some are sent down to the Commandant of Justice—and many die.
translations.idiomatic: Lately common memorials trip petty statutes; the Commandant of Justice swallows them and men die.

sentences.zh: 〈師古曰：「以其所言爲不急而罪之也。」

translations.literal: 〈Yan's note: "Because their words were judged not urgent they were punished."〉
translations.idiomatic: Yan Shigu: punished for "non-urgent" speech.

sentences.zh: 〉自陽朔以來，天下以言爲諱，朝廷尤甚，〈師古曰：「防人之口，法禁嚴切。」

translations.literal: 〉Since Yangshuo the realm has taken speech as taboo, worst at court—〈Yan's note: "They plugged men's mouths; the statutes bit deep."〉
translations.idiomatic: Since Yangshuo China fears its own tongue—worst in the hall—Yan Shigu: muzzling law.

sentences.zh: 〉羣臣皆承順上指，莫有執正。

translations.literal: 〉the host of ministers all follow the ruler's finger; none holds to the straight.
translations.idiomatic: Every minister nods at your hint; none stands straight.

sentences.zh: 何以明其然也？

translations.literal: How may one know it is so?
translations.idiomatic: How do we know?

sentences.zh: 取民所上書，陛下之所善，試下之廷尉，廷尉必曰「非所宜言，大不敬。」

translations.literal: Take what the people memorialize that Your Majesty praises, try sending it down to the Commandant of Justice—the Commandant will surely say, "Improper speech—great irreverence."
translations.idiomatic: Take a memorial you praised and give it to the judge—he will call it lese-majeste.

sentences.zh: 以此卜之，一矣。

translations.literal: Judge by this—one proof.
translations.idiomatic: That is one proof.

sentences.zh: 故京兆尹王章資質忠直，敢面引廷爭，孝元皇帝擢之，以厲具臣而矯曲朝。

translations.literal: Therefore Wang Zhang, former Governor of the Capital, by nature loyal and straight, dared face the court and argue in the open; Emperor Yuan raised him to warn placeholder ministers and straighten the bent court.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Zhang of the capital spoke truth to power; Yuan promoted him to shame seat-warmers.

sentences.zh: 〈師古曰：「具臣，具位之臣無益者也。

translations.literal: 〈Yan's note: "Ju chen means ministers who hold place without benefit.
translations.idiomatic: Yan Shigu: ju chen warms a useless chair.

sentences.zh: 矯，正也。」

translations.literal: Jiao means to correct."〉
translations.idiomatic: Jiao means set right."

sentences.zh: 〉及至陛下，戮及妻子。

translations.literal: 〉Under Your Majesty execution reached wife and children.
translations.idiomatic: Under you his wife and children died too.

sentences.zh: 且惡惡止其身，王章非有反畔之辜，而殃及家。

translations.literal: Moreover to hate evil stops at the person's body—Wang Zhang had no crime of rebellion, yet calamity reached his household.
translations.idiomatic: Punish the man, not the clan—Wang Zhang was no traitor, yet his house fell.

sentences.zh: 折直士之節，結諫臣之舌，羣臣皆知其非，然不敢爭，天下以言爲戒，最國家之大患也。

translations.literal: You have broken straight ministers' integrity and tied remonstrants' tongues; the host of ministers all know it is wrong yet dare not contend—the realm takes speech as warning: that is the state's greatest peril.
translations.idiomatic: You broke honest spines and sealed loyal mouths; all know it is wrong yet stay mute—speech taboo is the worst peril.

sentences.zh: 願陛下循高祖之軌，杜亡秦之路，〈師古曰：「杜，塞也。」

translations.literal: I beg Your Majesty follow Gaozu's track and block Qin's road to ruin—〈Yan's note: "Du means to stop up."〉
translations.idiomatic: Return to Gaozu's road and dam Qin's ditch—Yan Shigu: du is block.

sentences.zh: 〉數御十月之歌，〈孟康曰：「福譏切王氏。

translations.literal: 〉often take up the song of the tenth month—〈Meng Kang's note: "Fu satirizes the Wang clan.
translations.idiomatic: Sing often the "Tenth Month" ode—Meng Kang says Fu attacks the Wangs.

sentences.zh: 十月之詩，刺后族太盛也。」

translations.literal: The tenth month's poem satirizes the queen clan growing too strong."〉
translations.idiomatic: It warns when maternal kin swell."

sentences.zh: 師古曰：「詩小雅十月之交篇也。」

translations.literal: Yan's note: "It is the 'Shi jiao' piece in the Lesser Ya."〉
translations.idiomatic: Yan Shigu: from Lesser Ya "Shi yue zhi jiao."

sentences.zh: 〉留意亡逸之戒，〈師古曰：「周書篇名也，周公作之以戒成王。」

translations.literal: 〉heed the warning of "No Yi"—〈Yan's note: "A chapter title in the Zhou Documents, composed by the Duke of Zhou to warn King Cheng."〉
translations.idiomatic: Study the "Wu yi" chapter—Yan Shigu: Zhou document to King Cheng.

sentences.zh: 〉除不急之法，下亡諱之詔，博覽兼聽，謀及疏賤，令深者不隱，遠者不塞，所謂「辟四門，明四目」也。

translations.literal: 〉abolish statutes that are not urgent, issue an edict forbidding no speech, read widely and listen broadly, take counsel even from the distant and base, so the deep are not hidden and the far are not blocked—what is called "open the four gates, brighten the four eyes."
translations.idiomatic: Scrap petty speech laws, invite plain words, hear low and distant voices—open four gates, brighten four eyes.

sentences.zh: 〈師古曰：「虞書舜典曰『闢四門，明四目』，言開四門以致衆賢，則明視於四方也。」

translations.literal: 〈Yan's note: "The Shun dian in the Documents says 'open the four gates, brighten the four eyes'—meaning open four gates to draw many worthies and see clearly in four directions."〉
translations.idiomatic: Yan Shigu cites Shun's canon on opening the court to sight.

sentences.zh: 〉且不急之法，誹謗之微者也。

translations.literal: 〉Moreover statutes that are not urgent are the fine meshes of the slander laws.
translations.idiomatic: Petty statutes are slander-law snares.

sentences.zh: 「往者不可及，來者猶可追。」

translations.literal: "What is past cannot be caught up; what comes may still be pursued."
translations.idiomatic: The past is gone; the future can be mended.

sentences.zh: 方今君命犯而主威奪，〈師古曰：「君命犯者，謂大臣犯君之命。」

translations.literal: Now lordly commands are violated and sovereign awe is wrested away—〈Yan's note: "Lordly commands violated means great ministers defying the ruler's orders."〉
translations.idiomatic: Ministers defy edicts and imperial awe fades—Yan Shigu explains.

sentences.zh: 〉外戚之權日以益隆，陛下不見其形，願察其景。

translations.literal: 〉maternal kin's power grows daily greater; Your Majesty does not see the form—please observe the shadow.
translations.idiomatic: Consort power swells by the day—read the shadow if not the shape.

sentences.zh: 建始以來，日食地震，以率言之，三倍春秋，水災亡與比數。

translations.literal: Since Jianshi, by rough reckoning, solar eclipses and earthquakes have come three times the Spring and Autumn Annals' rate; floods cannot be matched in number.
translations.idiomatic: Since Jianshi, eclipses and quakes triple the Annals' count; floods beyond reckoning.

sentences.zh: 〈師古曰：「言其極多，不可比較而數也。」

translations.literal: 〈Yan's note: "It means they are extremely many—not to be compared or counted."〉
translations.idiomatic: Yan Shigu: too many to tally.

sentences.zh: 〉陰盛陽微，金鐵爲飛，此何景也！

translations.literal: 〉Yin waxes and yang wanes; metal and iron fly aloft—what shadow is this!
translations.idiomatic: Yin swells, yang thins—metal flies from forges: what omen is that!

sentences.zh: 〈張晏曰：「河平二年，沛郡鐵官鑄鐵如星飛上去，權臣用事之異也。」

translations.literal: 〈Zhang Yan's note: "In the second year of Heping, molten iron at the Pei foundry flew like stars—an anomaly of mighty ministers at work."〉
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Yan cites molten iron flying at Pei in Heping 2 as a sign of overmighty subjects.

sentences.zh: 蘇林曰：「『言之不從，是謂不艾，則金不從革。』

translations.literal: Su Lin said: "'If words are not heeded, that is called not governing—then metal will not obey smelting.'"
translations.idiomatic: Su Lin cites the Hong fan: disobedience in counsel skews the metal phase.

sentences.zh: 景，象也。

translations.literal: Jing means image.
translations.idiomatic: Jing means the portent or shadow cast.

sentences.zh: 何象，言將危亡也。」

translations.literal: What image? It speaks of imminent peril and ruin."
translations.idiomatic: The shadow foretells collapse."

sentences.zh: 〉漢興以來，社稷二危。

translations.literal: 〉Since Han rose, the altars of soil and grain have twice stood in peril.
translations.idiomatic: Since Gaozu the dynastic altars have twice tottered.

sentences.zh: 呂、霍、上官皆母后之家也，親親之道，全之爲右，〈師古曰：「務全安之，此爲上。」

translations.literal: The Lu, Huo, and Shangguan houses were all the empress dowagers' kin; in the way of loving kin, to keep them whole is best—〈Yan's note: "Strive to keep them secure—this is highest."〉
translations.idiomatic: The Lu, Huo, and Shangguan clans were maternal relatives; kin should be sheltered—Yan Shigu says keeping them safe ranks first.

sentences.zh: 〉當與之賢師良傅，敎以忠孝之道。

translations.literal: 〉One should give them worthy teachers and good tutors, instructing them in the way of loyalty and filial piety.
translations.idiomatic: Give them wise tutors and drill loyalty and filial piety.

sentences.zh: 今迺尊寵其位，授以魁柄，〈師古曰：「以斗爲喻也，斗身爲魁。」

translations.literal: Now you instead honor and dote on their stations and hand them the dipper's handle—〈Yan's note: "Using the dipper as metaphor—the dipper's body is the kui."〉
translations.idiomatic: Instead you fatten their offices and hand them the Big Dipper's handle—Yan Shigu compares power to the ladle stars.

sentences.zh: 〉使之驕逆，至於夷滅，〈師古曰：「夷，平也，謂平除之。」

translations.literal: 〉letting them grow arrogant and rebellious until they are leveled and exterminated—〈Yan's note: "Yi means level—utterly cleared."〉
translations.idiomatic: Until they turn arrogant and are wiped root and branch—Yan Shigu glosses yi as level away.

sentences.zh: 〉此失親親之大者也。

translations.literal: 〉this is the great failure in loving kin.
translations.idiomatic: That is how loving kin goes wrong.

sentences.zh: 自霍光之賢，不能爲子孫慮，故權臣易世則危。

translations.literal: Even Huo Guang's worth could not plan for sons and grandsons—therefore when power ministers change reigns, danger follows.
translations.idiomatic: Even Huo Guang could not save his line—so when regents survive a change of throne, peril follows.

sentences.zh: 書曰：「毋若火，始庸庸。」

translations.literal: The Document says, "Do not be like fire, which at first smolders."
translations.idiomatic: The Classic warns: do not let power smolder like kindling fire.

sentences.zh: 〈師古曰：「周書洛誥之辭也。

translations.literal: 〈Yan's note: "It is a saying in the Zhou document 'Luo gao.'"
translations.idiomatic: Yan Shigu: from the Luo gao in the Zhou Documents.

sentences.zh: 庸庸，微小貌也。

translations.literal: Yong yong means the appearance of tiny.
translations.idiomatic: Yong yong describes a tiny spark.

sentences.zh: 言火始微小，不早撲滅則至熾盛。

translations.literal: It means fire at first is small; if you do not stamp it out early it reaches blazing strength.
translations.idiomatic: A spark ignored becomes an inferno.

sentences.zh: 大臣貴擅，亦當早圖黜其權也。」

translations.literal: When great ministers grow noble and overbearing, you should early plan to strip their power."
translations.idiomatic: When ministers swell, cut their power early."

sentences.zh: 〉埶陵於君，權隆於主，然後防之，亦亡及已。

translations.literal: 〉When their power overtops the ruler and their authority looms above the sovereign, then you try to fence them—it is also too late.
translations.idiomatic: Wait until they dwarf the throne and it is too late to fence them in.

sentences.zh: 〈師古曰：「已，語終辭。」〉

translations.literal: 〈Yan's note: "Yi is a closing particle."〉
translations.idiomatic: Yan Shigu: yi closes the sentence.

sentences.zh: 上遂不納。

translations.literal: Thereupon the emperor did not accept.
translations.idiomatic: The throne ignored the memorial.

sentences.zh: 成帝乆亡繼嗣，福以爲宜建三統，封孔子之世以爲殷後，復上書曰：

translations.literal: Emperor Cheng long lacked an heir; Fu thought it fit to establish the three lineages, enfeoffing Confucius's descendants as Yin successors, and again submitted a memorial saying:
translations.idiomatic: Cheng still had no heir; Mei Fu urged reviving the three royal lines by enfeoffing Confucius's house as heir to Yin, and wrote again:

sentences.zh: 臣聞「不在其位，不謀其政」。

translations.literal: Your servant has heard, "Not in that office, do not seek that government."
translations.idiomatic: The classic says: out of office, out of policy.

sentences.zh: 政者職也，位卑而言高者罪也。

translations.literal: Government is duty; to speak high from a low station is a crime.
translations.idiomatic: Policy belongs to rank; humble men who lecture upward commit a crime.

sentences.zh: 越職觸罪，危言世患，雖伏質橫分，臣之願也。

translations.literal: To overstep duty and touch crime, to speak dangerously of the age's ills—though I lie split on the block, it is your servant's wish.
translations.idiomatic: I risk the waist block to name this age's sickness—gladly.

sentences.zh: 〈師古曰：「伏質，斬刑也。

translations.literal: 〈Yan's note: "Fu zhi is the beheading stroke."
translations.idiomatic: Yan Shigu: fu zhi is execution.

sentences.zh: 橫分，謂身首分離也。」

translations.literal: Heng fen means body and head parted."〉
translations.idiomatic: Heng fen means cut in two."

sentences.zh: 〉守職不言，沒齒身全，死之日，尸未腐而名滅，雖有景公之位，伏歷千駟，臣不貪也。

translations.literal: 〉To keep office and not speak, to die with teeth intact—on the day of death, before the corpse rots the name is gone; though you had Duke Jing's station and a thousand four-horse teams beneath the shaft, your servant would not covet it.
translations.idiomatic: Silent clerks die whole yet nameless before the flesh cools; I would not trade this for Qi Jing's thousand teams.

sentences.zh: 〈師古曰：「景公，齊景公也。

translations.literal: 〈Yan's note: "Duke Jing is Duke Jing of Qi."
translations.idiomatic: Yan Shigu identifies Qi Jinggong.

sentences.zh: 論語云：『齊景公有馬千駟，死之日，民無得而稱焉。』

translations.literal: The Analects says, "Duke Jing of Qi had a thousand teams of four horses, yet the day he died the people found nothing to praise him for."
translations.idiomatic: The Analects says Jing's thousand teams won him no praise after death.

sentences.zh: 故引之也。」

translations.literal: Therefore it is cited."〉
translations.idiomatic: Hence the quotation."

sentences.zh: 〉故願壹登文石之陛，涉赤墀之塗，〈應劭曰：「以丹淹泥塗殿上也。」

translations.literal: 〉Therefore I wish once to ascend the patterned stone steps and tread the crimson courtyard's mud—〈Ying Shao's note: "They smeared the hall with cinnabar mud."〉
translations.idiomatic: I beg once to climb the marble stairs and the cinnabar court—Ying Shao notes the red-daubed floor.

sentences.zh: 〉當戶牖之法坐，〈師古曰：「戶牖之間謂之扆，言負扆也。

translations.literal: 〉to sit at the lawful seat between the doors—〈Yan's note: "The space between doors is called yi—meaning you lean on the yi screen.
translations.idiomatic: To sit at the proper throne between the pillars—Yan Shigu names the yi screen behind the ruler.

sentences.zh: 法坐，正坐也，聽朝之處，猶言法官、法駕也。

translations.literal: Fa zuo means the correct seat, the place of hearing court—like saying fa guan, fa jia."
translations.idiomatic: Fa zuo is the formal audience seat, like "law carriage."

sentences.zh: 坐音才卧反。」

translations.literal: Zuo is read cai-wo fan."〉
translations.idiomatic: Fanqie for zuo."

sentences.zh: 〉盡平生之愚慮。

translations.literal: 〉and exhaust your servant's foolish thoughts.
translations.idiomatic: And pour out every thought I have.

sentences.zh: 亡益於時，有遺於世，〈師古曰：「遺，留也。」

translations.literal: If it brings no benefit to the times yet leaves something for posterity—〈Yan's note: "Yi means to leave behind."〉
translations.idiomatic: If it helps the age not at all yet leaves a trace—Yan Shigu: yi is leave behind.

sentences.zh: 〉此臣寢所以不安，食所以忘味也。

translations.literal: 〉that is why your servant's sleep is not peaceful and his food loses taste.
translations.idiomatic: That is why I cannot sleep or taste my food.

sentences.zh: 願陛下深省臣言。

translations.literal: I beg Your Majesty deeply examine your servant's words.
translations.idiomatic: I beg you weigh every line.

sentences.zh: 〈師古曰：「省，察也。」〉

translations.literal: 〈Yan's note: "Xing means to examine."〉
translations.idiomatic: Yan Shigu: xing is examine.

sentences.zh: 臣聞存人所以自立也，壅人所以自塞也。

translations.literal: Your servant has heard that preserving others is the means to establish oneself, and blocking others is the means to block oneself.
translations.idiomatic: Help others rise and you rise; choke them and you choke yourself.

sentences.zh: 善惡之報，各如其事。

translations.literal: The requital of good and evil matches each deed.
translations.idiomatic: Good and evil reap their own fruit.

sentences.zh: 昔者秦滅二周，夷六國，〈師古曰：「二周，東周、西周君也。

translations.literal: Formerly Qin destroyed the two Zhous and leveled the six states—〈Yan's note: "The two Zhous are the lords of Eastern and Western Zhou.
translations.idiomatic: Qin snuffed Eastern and Western Zhou and six kingdoms—Yan Shigu names the Zhou royal houses.

sentences.zh: 六國，齊、楚、韓、魏、趙、燕。」

translations.literal: The six states are Qi, Chu, Han, Wei, Zhao, Yan."〉
translations.idiomatic: The six are Qi, Chu, Han, Wei, Zhao, Yan."

sentences.zh: 〉隱士不顯，佚民不舉，〈師古曰：「佚與逸同。」

translations.literal: 〉recluses were not displayed, wandering men were not raised—〈Yan's note: "Yi is the same as yi 'ease' in the sense of reclusion."〉
translations.idiomatic: Hermits went unhonored, wanderers uncalled—Yan Shigu reads yi as reclusion.

sentences.zh: 〉絕三統，滅天道，是以身危子殺，厥孫不嗣，〈張晏曰：「身爲燕丹、張良所謀，子二世見殺。

translations.literal: 〉cut off the three lineages and extinguished Heaven's way—therefore his person was endangered, his son killed, his grandson could not succeed—〈Zhang Yan's note: "His person was plotted against by Jing Dan and Zhang Liang; his son the Second Generation was killed.
translations.idiomatic: Qin severed the three royal lines and snuffed Heaven's pattern—so the First Emperor fell to plots, Ershi died, Ying could not inherit—Zhang Yan glosses the line.

sentences.zh: 孫謂子嬰。」

translations.literal: The grandson means Ziying."〉
translations.idiomatic: The grandson is Ziying."

sentences.zh: 〉所謂壅人以自塞者也。

translations.literal: 〉this is what is called blocking others and thereby blocking oneself.
translations.idiomatic: That is choking others and choking yourself.

sentences.zh: 故武王克殷，未下車，存五帝之後，封殷於宋，紹夏於杞，〈師古曰：「謂封黃帝之後於薊，帝堯之後於祝，帝舜之後於陳，并杞、宋，是爲五帝。」

translations.literal: Therefore when King Wu conquered Yin, before he stepped down from the chariot he preserved the heirs of the Five Thearchs, enfeoffed Yin in Song and continued Xia in Qi—〈Yan's note: "He means enfeoffing Huang Di's heir at Ji, Yao's at Zhu, Shun's at Chen, together with Qi and Song—making five."〉
translations.idiomatic: King Wu, still in his chariot, restored the five royal lines—Song for Yin, Qi for Xia—Yan Shigu lists the five enfeoffments.

sentences.zh: 〉明著三統，示不獨有也。

translations.literal: 〉thereby clarifying the three lineages and showing he did not monopolize.
translations.idiomatic: He showed the three lines of succession so no one house owned Heaven.

sentences.zh: 是以姬姓半天下，遷廟之主，流出於戶，〈李竒曰：「言其多。」

translations.literal: Therefore the Ji surname held half the realm; wandering ancestral tablets flowed out the doors—〈Li Qi's note: "It means they were very many."〉
translations.idiomatic: So half the realm bore the Ji name and portable shrines crowded every gate—Li Qi stresses their number.

sentences.zh: 〉所謂存人以自立者也。

translations.literal: 〉this is what is called preserving others to establish oneself.
translations.idiomatic: That is helping others to stand firm yourself.

sentences.zh: 今成湯不祀，殷人亡後。

translations.literal: Now Cheng Tang receives no sacrifice and Yin has no successor.
translations.idiomatic: King Tang's line lacks smoke on the altar; Yin has no heir.

sentences.zh: 陛下繼嗣乆微，殆爲此也。

translations.literal: Your Majesty's heir has long been scarce—perhaps it is because of this.
translations.idiomatic: Your own heir-lessness may stem from the same neglect.

sentences.zh: 春秋經曰：「宋殺其大夫。」

translations.literal: The Spring and Autumn Annals records, "Song put its great officer to death."
translations.idiomatic: The Annals record Song executing a great officer.

sentences.zh: 穀梁傳曰：「其不稱名姓，以其在祖位，尊之也。」

translations.literal: The Guliang commentary says, "It does not give name or surname because he stood in the ancestor's place—therefore he is honored."
translations.idiomatic: Guliang says the victim went unnamed because he sat in the ancestral seat—honored silence.

sentences.zh: 〈師古曰：「事在僖二十五年。

translations.literal: 〈Yan's note: "The affair is in Xi 25.
translations.idiomatic: Yan Shigu: the entry falls in Xi 25.

sentences.zh: 穀梁所云『在祖位』者，謂孔子本宋孔父之後，防叔奔魯，遂爲魯人。

translations.literal: What the Guliang calls "in the ancestor's place" means Confucius was originally descended from Song's Kong Fu; Fangshu fled to Lu and became a Lu man.
translations.idiomatic: The "ancestor seat" means Confucius sprang from Song's Kong clan before Fangshu fled to Lu.

sentences.zh: 今宋所殺者亦孔父之後留在宋者，於孔子爲祖列，故尊而不名也。」

translations.literal: Now the man Song killed was also Kong Fu's descendant who remained in Song—in the ancestor column to Confucius, hence honored without naming."〉
translations.idiomatic: The victim was another Kong kinsman left in Song—Confucius's senior line—hence the text withholds his name."

sentences.zh: 〉此言孔子故殷後也，雖不正統，封其子孫以爲殷後，禮亦宜之。

translations.literal: 〉This speech means Confucius was indeed Yin's descendant; though not the direct succession, to enfeoff his posterity as Yin's heir is also ritually proper.
translations.idiomatic: The classic hints Confucius carried Yin blood; even off the main line, enfeoffing his seed as Yin heir fits the rites.

sentences.zh: 何者？

translations.literal: Why?
translations.idiomatic: Why?

sentences.zh: 諸侯奪宗，聖庶奪適。

translations.literal: Feudal lords displace the main lineage; sage-born younger sons displace the heir.
translations.idiomatic: Marquises can supplant the main house; wise younger sons can supplant the heir.

sentences.zh: 〈如淳曰：「奪宗，始封之君尊爲諸侯，則奪其舊爲宗子之事也。

translations.literal: 〈Ru Chun's note: "Duo zong means the first enfeoffed lord, honored as marquis, thereby strips the old duty of being lineage heir.
translations.idiomatic: Ru Chun: a new marquis may displace the old zongzi role.

sentences.zh: 奪適，文王舍伯邑考而立武王是也。

translations.literal: Duo shi means King Wen passed over Bo Yikao and established King Wu."
translations.idiomatic: Duo shi is Wen passing Bo Yikao for Wu."

sentences.zh: 孔子雖庶，可爲殷後。」

translations.literal: Confucius, though born a younger son, may serve as Yin's heir."〉
translations.idiomatic: Confucius, though a cadet branch, may stand as Yin heir."

sentences.zh: 師古曰：「適讀曰嫡。」

translations.literal: Yan's note: "Shi is read di."〉
translations.idiomatic: Yan Shigu: shi reads di, heir.

sentences.zh: 〉傳曰「賢者子孫宜有土」，而況聖人，又殷之後哉！

translations.literal: 〉The tradition says "the worthy man's descendants deserve land"—how much the more a sage who is also Yin's kin!
translations.idiomatic: The canon says worthy lines deserve fiefs—how much more a sage of Yin stock!

sentences.zh: 昔成王以諸侯禮葬周公，而皇天動威，雷風著災。

translations.literal: Formerly King Cheng buried the Duke of Zhou with a feudal lord's rites, and High Heaven stirred its might—thunder and wind showed disaster.
translations.idiomatic: When Cheng buried the Duke of Zhou with only a lord's honors, Heaven answered with storm and wind.

sentences.zh: 〈師古曰：「尚書大傳云：『周公疾，曰：「吾死必葬於成周，示天下臣於成王也。」

translations.literal: 〈Yan's note: "The Shang shu da zhuan says: when the Duke of Zhou fell ill he said, 'When I die bury me at Chengzhou to show the world I am subject to King Cheng.'"
translations.idiomatic: Yan Shigu quotes the Great Tradition: Zhou asked burial at Chengzhou to show he served Cheng.

sentences.zh: 周公死，天乃雷雨以風，禾盡偃，大木斯拔。

translations.literal: When the Duke of Zhou died, Heaven then sent thunder, rain, and wind—the grain all lay flat, great trees were torn up.
translations.idiomatic: At his death Heaven blasted wind and rain until grain flattened and trees uprooted.

sentences.zh: 國恐，王與大夫開金縢之書，執書以泣曰：「周公勤勞王家，予幼人弗及知。」

translations.literal: The state was afraid; the king with the great officers opened the metal-bound casket, took the document and wept, saying, "The Duke of Zhou was diligent for the royal house—I, a child, did not know it in time."
translations.idiomatic: Panic gripped the court; king and ministers opened the golden coffer and wept that they had missed Zhou's loyalty.

sentences.zh: 乃不葬於成周而葬之於畢，示天下不敢臣。』」

translations.literal: Then he was not buried at Chengzhou but at Bi, to show the world they dared not treat him as subject."'"
translations.idiomatic: They moved the tomb to Bi to show they dared not treat him as a vassal."

sentences.zh: 〉今仲尼之廟不出闕里，〈師古曰：「闕里，孔子舊里也。

translations.literal: 〉Now Confucius's temple does not step beyond Que village—〈Yan's note: "Que village is Confucius's old home."
translations.idiomatic: Today Kong's shrine never leaves Queli—Yan Shigu names his lane.

sentences.zh: 言除此之外，更無祭祀孔子者也。」

translations.literal: It means beyond that there is no other place sacrificing to Confucius."〉
translations.idiomatic: No second site honors the sage."

sentences.zh: 〉孔氏子孫不免編戶，〈師古曰：「列爲庶人也。」

translations.literal: 〉Kong descendants still rank as registered households—〈Yan's note: "They are listed as commoners."〉
translations.idiomatic: His kin still pay poll tax as commoners—Yan Shigu notes their mean status.

sentences.zh: 〉以聖人而歆匹夫之祀，非皇天之意也。

translations.literal: 〉yet the sage receives only a commoner's offerings—this is not High Heaven's intent.
translations.idiomatic: A sage fed like a peasant cannot be Heaven's will.

sentences.zh: 今陛下誠能據仲尼之素功，以封其子孫，〈師古曰：「素功，素王之功也。

translations.literal: Now if Your Majesty will truly take Confucius's uncrowned merit and enfeoff his descendants—〈Yan's note: "Su gong means the merit of the uncrowned king."
translations.idiomatic: If you reward Confucius's "uncrowned king" merit with a fief—Yan Shigu cites the Guliang title.

sentences.zh: 穀梁傳曰『孔子素王』。」

translations.literal: The Guliang says "Confucius was the uncrowned king."'"
translations.idiomatic: Guliang calls him the king without throne."

sentences.zh: 〉則國家必獲其福，又陛下之名與天亡極。

translations.literal: 〉then the state will surely receive its blessing, and Your Majesty's name will match Heaven without limit.
translations.idiomatic: The dynasty will gain blessing and your name will ride the sky.

sentences.zh: 何者？

translations.literal: Why?
translations.idiomatic: Why?

sentences.zh: 追聖人素功，封其子孫，未有法也，後聖必以爲則。

translations.literal: To pursue a sage's uncrowned merit and enfeoff his posterity sets no precedent—later sages will surely take it as model.
translations.idiomatic: Honoring a throneless sage sets precedent for every age to come.

sentences.zh: 不滅之名，可不勉哉！

translations.literal: A name that is not extinguished—can you not strive for it!
translations.idiomatic: Immortal fame is worth the reach.

sentences.zh: 福孤遠，又譏切王氏，故終不見納。

translations.literal: Fu was isolated and far, again cutting against the Wang clan, and therefore in the end was never accepted.
translations.idiomatic: Mei Fu stood alone, slashed at the Wangs, and was never heard.

sentences.zh: 至元始中，王莽顓政，〈師古曰：「顓讀與專同。」

translations.literal: By the Yanshi era Wang Mang monopolized government—〈Yan's note: "Zhuan is read the same as zhuan."〉
translations.idiomatic: In Yanshi Wang Mang seized the government—Yan Shigu reads zhuan as monopolize.

sentences.zh: 〉福一朝棄妻子，去九江，至今傳以爲仙。

translations.literal: 〉Fu in one morning abandoned wife and children, left Jiujiang, and to this day is handed down as an immortal.
translations.idiomatic: He abandoned kin overnight, fled to Jiujiang, and legend makes him a transcendent.

sentences.zh: 其後，人有見福於會稽者，變名姓，爲吳市門卒云。

translations.literal: Afterward someone saw Fu in Kuaiji; he had changed his name and served as gate guard of Wu market, it is said.
translations.idiomatic: Later rumor placed him in Kuaiji under a new name, tending a market gate.

sentences.zh: 〈師古曰：「其後謂棄妻子去之後。」〉

translations.literal: 〈Yan's note: "Afterward means after he abandoned wife and children and left."〉
translations.idiomatic: Yan Shigu: afterward means after his flight.

sentences.zh: 云敞字幼孺，平陵人也。

translations.literal: Yun Chang, courtesy name Youru, was a man of Pingling.
translations.idiomatic: Yun Chang, courtesy Youru, came from Pingling.

sentences.zh: 師事同縣吳章，章治尚書經爲博士。

translations.literal: He took as teacher Wu Zhang of the same county; Zhang studied the Documents classic and became an erudite.
translations.idiomatic: He studied under Wu Zhang of his county; Zhang mastered the Shang shu and rose to erudite.

sentences.zh: 平帝以中山王即帝位，年幼，莽秉政，自號安漢公。

translations.literal: When Emperor Ping from Zhongshan took the throne, being young, Mang held power and styled himself Duke Who Pacifies Han.
translations.idiomatic: Boy emperor Ping of Zhongshan left Mang as regent calling himself the Han-pacifying duke.

sentences.zh: 以平帝爲成帝後，不得顧私親，帝母及外家衞氏皆留中山，不得至京師。

translations.literal: Taking Ping as heir to Cheng, he could not heed private kin; the emperor's mother and the Wei maternal kin all remained in Zhongshan and could not come to the capital.
translations.idiomatic: As Cheng's heir he barred his mother and the Wei in-laws from the capital.

sentences.zh: 莽長子宇，非莽鬲絕衞氏，〈師古曰：「鬲讀與隔同。」

translations.literal: Mang's eldest son Yu resented Mang for blocking the Wei—〈Yan's note: "Ge is read the same as ge."〉
translations.idiomatic: Mang's heir Yu hated isolating the Wei clan—Yan Shigu reads ge as block.

sentences.zh: 〉恐帝長大後見怨。

translations.literal: 〉fearing that when the emperor grew he would harbor resentment.
translations.idiomatic: He feared the boy emperor's future grudge.

sentences.zh: 宇與吳章謀，夜以血塗莽門，若鬼神之戒，兾以懼莽。

translations.literal: Yu plotted with Wu Zhang, smearing blood on Mang's gate by night as if spirits warned, hoping thereby to frighten Mang.
translations.idiomatic: Yu and Zhang smeared Mang's gate with blood like a ghost warning.

sentences.zh: 章欲因對其咎。

translations.literal: Zhang wished thereby to get a chance to speak his blame.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang meant to use the scare to open a remonstrance.

sentences.zh: 事發覺，莽殺宇，誅滅衞氏，謀所聯及，死者百餘人。

translations.literal: When the affair was discovered, Mang killed Yu, exterminated the Wei, and those implicated in the plot—more than a hundred died.
translations.idiomatic: The plot surfaced; Mang killed Yu, wiped the Wei, and executed over a hundred linked names.

sentences.zh: 章坐要斬，磔尸東市門。

translations.literal: Zhang sat guilty of waist-cutting; his corpse was exposed at the eastern market gate.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang died by waist-slicing; his body was displayed at the east market.

sentences.zh: 初，章爲當世名儒，敎授尤盛，弟子千餘人，莽以爲惡人黨，皆當禁錮，不得仕宦。

translations.literal: At first Zhang was a famous scholar of the age; his teaching was especially flourishing—more than a thousand disciples—and Mang deemed his faction evil; all were to be barred and forbidden office.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang had been a celebrated teacher with a thousand pupils; Mang branded the school criminal and barred every man from office.

sentences.zh: 門人盡更名他師。

translations.literal: The disciples all changed names and took other teachers.
translations.idiomatic: Students renounced him overnight and claimed new masters.

sentences.zh: 〈師古曰：「更以他人爲師，諱不言是章弟子。」

translations.literal: 〈Yan's note: "They took someone else as teacher, ashamed to say they were Zhang's disciples."〉
translations.idiomatic: Yan Shigu: they hid ever having studied under Zhang.

sentences.zh: 〉敞時爲大司徒掾，自劾吳章第子，收抱章尸歸，棺斂葬之，〈師古曰：「棺音工喚反。

translations.literal: At that time Chang was a clerk in the Grand Minister of Education's bureau; he impeached himself as Zhang's disciple, collected and embraced Zhang's corpse, returned home, coffined and buried him—〈Yan's note: "Guan is read gong-huan fan.
translations.idiomatic: Chang served as a clerk under the Grand Minister of Education; he confessed to being Zhang's pupil, reclaimed the corpse, and buried it—Yan Shigu gives fanqie for guan.

sentences.zh: 斂音力贍反。」

translations.literal: Lian is read li-shan fan."〉
translations.idiomatic: Lian is read li-shan."

sentences.zh: 〉京師稱焉。

translations.literal: 〉The capital praised him.
translations.idiomatic: Chang'an praised his courage.

sentences.zh: 車騎將軍王舜高其志節，比之欒布，表奏以爲掾，薦爲中郎諫大夫。
莽篡位，王舜爲太師。
復薦敞可輔職。
〈師古曰：「爲輔弼之任。」
〉以病免。
唐林言敞可典郡，擢爲魯郡大尹。
更始時，安車徵敞爲御史大夫，復病免去，卒于家。
贊曰：昔仲尼稱不得中行，則思狂狷。
〈師古曰：「論語載孔子曰：『不得中行而與之，必也狂狷乎！
狂者進取，狷者有所不爲。』
中行，中庸也。
狷，介也。
言不必得中庸之人與之論道，則思狂狷，猶愈於頑嚚無識者也。
狷音子掾反。」
〉觀楊王孫之志，賢於秦始皇遠矣。
世稱朱雲多過其實，故曰「蓋有不知而作之者，我亡是也。」
〈師古曰：「論語稱孔子之言也。
疾時人妄有述作，非有實也。」
〉胡建臨敵敢斷，武昭於外。
〈師古曰：「昭，明也。」
〉斬伐姦隙，軍旅不隊。
梅福之辭，合於大雅，雖無老成，尚有典刑；
殷監不遠，夏后所聞。
〈師古曰：「大雅蕩之詩曰『雖無老成人，尚有典刑』，言今雖無其人，尚有故法可案用也。
又曰『殷監不遠，在夏后之時』，言殷視夏桀之亡，可爲戒也。
贊引此者，謂梅福請封孔子後，是案武王克商之法而行之。
又視秦滅二周，夷六國，不爲立後，自取喪亡，可爲戒也。」
〉遂從所好，全性市門。
云敞之義，著於吳章，爲仁由己，再入大府，〈師古曰：「論語稱孔子曰：『爲仁由己，而由人乎哉！』
此贊引之。
再入大府，謂初爲大司徒掾，後爲車騎將軍掾也。」
〉清則濯纓，何遠之有？
〈師古曰：「楚辭漁父之歌曰：『滄浪之水清，可以濯我纓；
滄浪之水濁，可以濯我足。』
遇治則仕，遇亂則隱，云敞謝病去職，近於此義也。」〉
